'''Danger Level: Hazardous'''
'''>> Number Designation: 32 <<'''
'''>> Object Classification: Unbeneficial for Human Use <<'''
'''>> Location: Heavily Unstable Levels <<'''
'''Object 32''', known as '''corruptium''', or scientifically as ''Substum ins'', is an unstable and unpredictable substance found commonly in unstable and corrupted levels of the Backrooms. Up until February 22, 2026, '''Object 32''' was referred to as "corruptionite", a temporary placeholder name that has since been dropped in favor for its current more appropriate name.
'''Properties'''
{{Blurimage|align=center|file=TCI Corruptionite.jpg|hovertext=The substance of incomprehensibility|caption=An entire room covered with Corruptium.}} Contrary to popular belief, '''corruptium''' does not possess the ability to corrupt levels or mutate entities but is rather a byproduct of instability and corruption. '''Corruptium''' is extremely hot, reaching up to approximately 1,200°C or 2,192°F degrees, which is capable of giving wanderers 3rd-degree burns or fatal injuries in just seconds and is comparable to molten lava. It is generally believed that the instability, the high density, and the pressure are the reasons for its high temperature. Due to how hot '''corruptium''' is, storing the liquid in plastic cups or bottles by all means is not possible. Accordingly, it is best to store it in metallic cups or containers in a cool environment. '''Corruptium''' is very viscous, akin to ketchup, meaning one can easily escape the liquid if it is flowing to them. Furthermore, if '''corruptium''' successfully coats an entire object, the materials making up the object will be permanently replaced with the aforementioned '''corruptium'''. '''Corruptium''' can manifest as a liquid or a solid, but instances of it being a gas or plasma have not been reported. Unlike in liquid form, '''corruptium''' is mostly harmless in solid form, and will match the temperature of its surroundings. '''Corruptium''' only melts back to liquid form when heated pressure is applied to it.
'''Usage'''
Although unadvised, one possible application for '''corruptium''' could be using it as a weapon against entities. Otherwise, there are no cases where it should be used due to its hazardous properties.
'''Identification'''
'''corruptium''' can be identified via the heat it emits, which one can feel as far as 30 feet away from it. '''corruptium's''' appearance is interpreted as TV static, though more colorful. Moreover, '''corruptium''' can appear as rectangular blocks of flashing neon over grey, purple, blue, or violet. '''Corruptium''' allegedly has a bitter taste in solid form and a 'static' like taste in liquid form.
'''Effects and Symptoms'''
Consuming '''corruptium''' is highly advised against, as it is capable of killing lifeforms that do so in mere hours. The following symptoms and side effects of consuming the liquid are below:

'''Symptoms for Corruptium'''

As soon as a wanderer consumes liquid '''corruptium''', they will immediately die from the extreme lethal burns. However, if the '''corruptium''' is solid and cool, they are able to survive for much longer. Nonetheless, it is still fatal and as '''corruptium's''' symptoms are incurable, it is already too late to save a wanderer consuming the substance completely; one must choke the object out as soon as possible before it enters the digestive tract. The symptoms of consuming solid '''corruptium''' are listed below: ===Symptom 1 (~30 min.)=== If not choked out of the body, wanderers will endure headaches, stomach aches, and migraines within half an hour. Likewise, a burning sensation may be felt in the stomach. ===Symptom 2 (1.30 hr.)=== Headaches will amplify, as well as the migraines. The burning sensation in one's stomach will persist, and the victim may begin to vomit. Unfortunately, vomiting will only expel out a minuscule amount of '''corruptium'''. ===Symptom 3 (~2 hr.)=== Stomach aches will become much more painful, in addition to diarrhea and vomiting coming into the act. Furthermore, wanderers will begin to hallucinate. At this point, the victim will be extremely dehydrated, due to the liquid's ability to attract other liquids. ===Symptom 4 (~4 hr.)=== Wanderers will begin to experience schizophrenia and vomit out blood. The skin may start to peel off, as well as the victim now experiences chronic diarrhea. ===Symptom 5 (>4 hr.)=== This is the final symptom the victim will go through before death. The victim will now go through severe schizophrenia and respiratory problems, and the eyes will pop out. Additionally, the eyes will turn static, meaning the victim will no longer see any visual input. Eventually, the victim will inevitably cease to exist, and their body will implode into '''corruptium'''.
'''Locations'''
According to the Morrison Stability Scale, '''corruptium''' is to be found in levels with a stability, or rather instability, of 7.6 to 9.5; albeit, it is possible for '''corruptium''' to reside in more stable and normal levels. The following are levels where '''corruptium''' has been most prominently or has been theorized to be potentially found; levels that house '''corruptium''' but are not listed below must be included immediately for the wanderer's safety. *[[The Corrupted Instability]] - '''corruptium''' is dominant and widespread in its sub-levels, especially Sub-Level 1 and Sub-Level 5. *[[Waste Disposal]] - Like many other [[Object List|substances]], '''corruptium''' from numerous levels frequently no-clips into the level, causing it to rain down into the landscape.
'''Variants'''
Despite '''corruptium's''' apparent ability to attract and move certain liquids along with it, most of them in reality besides one particular substance cannot be mixed with it due to '''corruptium''' in addition to having the means of repelling those same liquids. Therefore, most liquids are unable to come into contact with '''corruptium'''.
'''Corruptivoid'''
{{Blurimage|align=center|file=LiquidVoid.jpg|hovertext=Someone spilt their drink...|caption=A body of Corruptivoid flooding a portion of a hallway.}} '''Corruptivoid''', or ''Substum vod'' as its scientific name is a unique variant of '''corruptium'''. It can be made via mixing an unspecified amount of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] with '''corruptium'''. This will cause a violent reaction and the aforementioned '''corruptium''' to turn entirely black, and change drastically in physical appearance and its properties. Regardless, it is commonly found naturally rather than being made. Conversely, '''corruptivoid''', is strikingly cooler, typically matching the room temperature of its surroundings. As a result, it is somewhat safer in contrast to '''corruptium''' and Liquid Pain. '''Corruptivoid''' has a gooey feeling to it and is very sticky. Hence, it is challenging to get off should it stick onto one's body. Additionally, '''corruptivoid''' appears noticeably reflective, sometimes acting closely to a mirror. Similar to its original form, '''corruptivoid''', if large enough, can cause wanderers standing directly on it to be slowly consumed by it. As previously mentioned, it can pose a difficulty to escape being trapped within the liquid, and depending on how deep the body of '''corruptivoid''', it may act as quicksand. In levels such as the first sub-level of [[The Corrupted Instability]], if '''corruptivoid''' manages to swallow an object entirely, the victim will reportedly be sent to [[The Void|the Void]]. A clear explanation is not determined, although as '''corruptivoid''' only possess this quirk in the sub-level, it most likely has to do with the sub-level's anomalous properties.
'''Corruptocolor'''
[[File:CorruptioniteLogo.png|thumb|223px|link=]]
'''Danger Level: Variable'''
'''>> Number Designation: 32-B <<'''
'''>> Object Classification: Can Range from Slight Utility to Hazardous<<'''
'''>> Location: Neon Themed Levels<<'''

A recently substance discovered that is related to '''Object 32''' is known as '''Object 32-B''', '''corruptocolor''', or ''Substum insolor'' by its scientific name. In stark contrast to '''corruptium''' or '''Corruptivoid''', '''corruptocolor'''it is a neon liquid that can shine and brighten well, with its appearance being described as "neon water". Unlike '''Corruptivoid''', '''corruptocolor''' cannot be artificially made and can only be found naturally. The appearance of '''corruptocolor''' can be either a single neon color that never changes, or as a neon color that changes its color at any time to any vibrant neon color. {{Blurimage|align=center|file=Level37.5-2.png|hovertext=Neon Water|caption=Corruptocolor mixes with pool water in [[Blue Horizon]].}} The usage of this substance is beneficial and can be usually found in levels with neon themes, where the substance is found in the light bulbs or other objects that may provide neon light in the area. Some levels that have pools, such as [[Blue Horizon]], can also contain this substance, with the substance found in the pool water mixing with water to create a neon pool that can light itself without any pool light. Because '''corruptocolor''' is capable of rapidly changing color, looking at the rapidly changing brightness can cause photosensitive epilepsy, a sort of seizure, for some people. This neurological condition affects how the brain reacts to visual stimuli like flashing lights or quickly shifting patterns. In levels such as [[Level 226]], as well as Skyan in [[Level 306]], '''corruptocolor''' constantly changes its dazzling patterns, colors, and flashing lights. This can cause seizures in anyone who stares at it. Seizures can result in dizziness or other symptoms, including confusion. A person may have memory loss, hallucinations, convulsions, loss of consciousness, or even die if the symptoms get worse after looking at the substance for a long time. {{Blurimage|align=center|file=Level-minus-22-2.jpg|hovertext=Glitch|caption=An image of [[Level 226]], where '''corruptocolor''' appears to be exceedingly bright and deadly.}}
'''Trivia'''
*'''Corruptium''' under a microscope looks blocky. *The type of black '''corruptivoid''' appears as is closer to matte black. However, due to how reflective it is, the true color of the liquid is difficult to see. *Because '''corruptocolor''' can manifest as a variety of other colors, it is commonly confused for other substances. {{Blurimage|align=center|file=Glitched_Earth_lol.png|hovertext=Flashing lights of instability|caption=A sample of Corruptium viewed under a microscope.}}
